pages.title: Faculty & Staff | Sacramento State
pages.description: Learn more about what services faculty and staff of Sac State's Career Center offer.
headings: Request a Presentation, Speaker or Tabling
Request Staff to Table or Speak at an Event
Connect with the Career Center Team
Best Practices for Collaborating with the Career Center
contentBlocks: Faculty, staff and student club/organization leaders can select from a variety of career-related presentation topics through the “Request a Live Presentation” link below. Live presentations are available during Career Center business hours of 8am – 5pm. Due to the high volume of presentations we receive, please request no more than two presentations for one course.
For presentations outside of business hours of 8am – 5pm, faculty, staff and student club/organization leaders may request a recorded presentation from a variety of topics.
For tabling, panels, and event speaker requests during business hours, please use the form below.
Request Staff to Table or Speak at an Event
Looking for more ways to integrate career development? Check out our best practices for collaboration below! For additional support, please contact careercenter@csus.edu .
Want to partner to embed career development and workforce development components into your curriculum or organization?
The Career Center can also serve as the connection between faculty and employers. Refer community members, and potential employers/internship hosts to our Employer Relations team.
We recommend the following report for information on helping students build momentum toward careers: Purpose First .
Connect with the Career Center Team for Questions

Use career fair and event (Spring Fairs: All Majors Career Fair is Mon., March 2nd and Education, Youth & Family Services Fair is Tues., March 3rd) participation as extra credit or class assignment opportunities (e.g. short reflection)
Add a link to the Career Center website as a student resource in your syllabus or Canvas page.
Assign mock interviews using Big Interview
Encourage students to explore major and career options via FOCUS2 Career and What Can I Do with This Major
Encourage students to research industries that interest them using Labor Market Insights , California Career Zone , O*Net and Library resources
Suggest that students use AI like ChatGPT to check resume and cover letter grammar and spelling
For students in need of extra career support, encourage an appointment for Career Center Counseling.
For other “College to Career Readiness” ideas for a career ready workforce, visit NACE
Join a Professional Learning Community or Teaching Institute in collaboration with CTL on how to help students be Career Ready
